program.name: STARLAB
program.fullName: Science Through Advanced Research, Learning, Application, and Building
program.tagline: Teacher-facing curriculum portal for launching, teaching, managing, assessing, and showcasing authentic student research.

Unit 1.title: Introduction to Scientific Research and Experimental Design
Unit 1.focus: Research culture, scientific inquiry, ethics, experimental design, topic brainstorming, and preliminary research questions.
Unit 1.workflow: Open the start-here guide, launch Decks 1-2, print Unit 1 handouts, and conference with students on early research questions.

Unit 2.title: Project Planning and Hypothesis Development
Unit 2.focus: Refining project ideas, background research, hypotheses or design criteria, procedures, data plans, safety, ethics, and approval.
Unit 2.workflow: Use the project approval system, Decks 3-5, and Unit 2 planning handouts before students begin pilot testing.

Unit 3.title: Pilot Testing and Data Collection
Unit 3.focus: Pilot testing, research journals, revised procedures, systematic data collection, troubleshooting, mentor feedback, and progress reporting.
Unit 3.workflow: Move week by week through pilot testing, quality checks, mentor feedback, and readiness for analysis.

Unit 4.title: Data Analysis and Interpretation
Unit 4.focus: Raw versus clean data, analysis method selection, graphing, uncertainty, limitations, claim-evidence-reasoning, and readiness for reporting.
Unit 4.workflow: Audit data first, select analysis methods, build visuals, and prepare interpretation notes for reports.

Unit 5.title: Scientific Reporting and Presentation Preparation
Unit 5.focus: Scientific report structure, introduction, methods, results, discussion, conclusion, peer review, visual aids, and presentation preparation.
Unit 5.workflow: Draft the report section by section, run peer review, then shift students into presentation design.

Unit 6.title: Project Presentation and Reflection
Unit 6.focus: Final presentation readiness, Q&A, showcase preparation, public presentation, reflection, portfolio, and next steps.
Unit 6.workflow: Prepare students for final defense, public showcase expectations, audience Q&A, and post-course reflection.

decks.title: Authentic Research and Course Launch
decks.teachingMoment: Launch the course and establish research culture.

decks.title: From Topic to Research Question
decks.teachingMoment: Help students turn interests into testable questions.

decks.title: Research Ethics, Safety, and Integrity
decks.teachingMoment: Frame ethics, safety, and responsible project choices.

decks.title: Experimental Design and Engineering Design Basics
decks.teachingMoment: Teach variables, controls, criteria, constraints, and design logic.

decks.title: Building a Formal Research Plan
decks.teachingMoment: Guide students into a complete plan for approval.

decks.title: Pilot Testing - Test Small First
decks.teachingMoment: Introduce small-scale testing before full data collection.

decks.title: Research Journals and Scientific Documentation
decks.teachingMoment: Set expectations for documentation and evidence trails.

decks.title: Troubleshooting and Responsible Project Pivots
decks.teachingMoment: Support iteration, problem solving, and justified pivots.

decks.title: Data Organization: Raw Data vs. Clean Data
decks.teachingMoment: Move students from collected data to analyzable data.

decks.title: Choosing the Right Data Analysis Method
decks.teachingMoment: Match research questions and data types to analysis methods.

decks.title: Graphing and Visual Ethics
decks.teachingMoment: Create honest, useful graphs and tables.

decks.title: Scientific Report Structure
decks.teachingMoment: Introduce the full research report architecture.

decks.title: Writing the Discussion: Claim, Evidence, Reasoning
decks.teachingMoment: Build rigorous discussion and interpretation writing.

decks.title: Designing a Research Poster or Presentation Board
Answering Questions Like a Scientist
decks.teachingMoment: Prepare students for Q&A and evidence defense.

decks.title: Final Reflection and Research Portfolio
decks.teachingMoment: Close the course with portfolio and reflection.
